metadata.notes: agent=yes daemon=bug (context-window bar unresolved: mistral not in capacity map) driver=gap:wait_turn

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug (model name displays; the context-window BAR does NOT render for mistral models)
driver_capability: gap:wait_turn
route: driver-gap

## Why this cell exists

1.8 is the first-contact visibility check (#599): after one trivial turn the dashboard must show WHICH model the session runs AND HOW FULL its context is. The scenario's acceptance is explicit that BOTH halves land — `metrics.model_name` non-empty/!=unknown, `metrics.context_window > 0`, and `metrics.context_utilization_percentage > 0`. For mistral-vibe the model half works and the context half does not, so this cell splits: the daemon is `bug` on the context-window bar.

## Agent (yes)

Vibe writes a sibling `meta.json` sidecar next to every `~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/messages.jsonl`, rewritten after each turn. `config.active_model` and `stats.context_tokens` are both populated from the first turn. Confirmed against the LIVE surface: 6 local sidecars all carry `active_model: "mistral-medium-3.5"` with `context_tokens` populated (e.g. 136662, 146631, 31309). So the agent exposes both metrics from first contact.

## Daemon (bug) — model displays, context bar does not

The vibe parser's `applySidecar` (parser.go L98-115) sets `ev.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(st.model)` on every event and `ev.Tokens = &TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens}` on `turn_done`. It deliberately does NOT set `ContextWindow` — the window is resolved downstream by `ComputeContextUtilization` (tailer_metrics.go L512-543) via the capacity map, keyed on the normalized model name.

Model name: `NormalizeModelName("mistral-medium-3.5")` returns `"mistral-medium-3.5"` verbatim (no alias transform — confirmed by running it) → non-empty, != unknown. The `metrics.model_name` acceptance bullet PASSES.

Context bar: `mistral-medium-3.5` is absent from the capacity context-window/alias map. The ONLY mistral entry in `core/pkg/capacity` is the CO2 tier (`co2.go`: `"mistral": {gCO2PerToken: 0.00285}`); there is no context-window entry and no alias in `aliases.go` for any mistral/medium/devstral/magistral model. So `capacityMgr.GetModelCapacity("mistral-medium-3.5").ContextWindow` returns 0, `ComputeContextUtilization` hits the guard at tailer_metrics.go L527-528 and returns `(0, 0, "unknown", true)` — `ContextWindow=0`, `ContextUtilization=0`, `ContextWindowUnknown=true`. Both `context_window > 0` and `context_utilization_percentage > 0` acceptance bullets FAIL. Because the scenario's acceptance REQUIRES the utilization bar, this is a spec-required observable the daemon mis-handles → daemon=bug (not a caveat). The fix is product/data — add mistral models (window + alias) to `core/pkg/capacity`; a known follow-up. Not `incapable`: the mechanism exists (Tokens + ModelName are emitted); only the capacity data is missing.

Cost: the sidecar also carries `stats.session_cost` + per-million prices, but the adapter does NOT emit cost (per-turn cost is not backfillable) — the spec does not assert `cost_nonzero`.

## Driver (gap:wait_turn)

The recipe elicits the behavior with `send` → `wait_turn` → `sleep`; the acceptance is checked AFTER the first turn completes, so `wait_turn` is load-bearing. mistral-vibe's `driver-interactive.sh` is freshly scaffolded: `DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"` and SEAM 2 (`wait_turn`, L168-170) is a loud `not_implemented` stub, so `recipe-lint`'s `driver_elicits_from_file` does NOT list `wait_turn` → any recipe needing it is a semantic gap. SEAM 3 (resolve session_id + transcript path, L158) is also a TODO stub, so the session contract currently records nothing. This is tooling work, not an observability limit: `record` ports SEAM 2 (+ SEAM 3) from the reference driver (e.g. drive-claudecode-interactive.sh) before it drives.

## Route: driver-gap

With daemon=bug AND driver=gap:*, the routing table lands on driver-gap: `record` ports the missing `wait_turn` step, then drives; the recording will exercise the daemon=bug (context bar) which is preserved as `known_failing` so `record` surfaces an issue: payload for the capacity-map follow-up. The recipe + spec are kept intact and assert the CORRECT (full) behavior.
caveats: known_failing daemon=bug is narrow: ONLY the context-window bar (context_window / context_utilization). Model name and token count both surface correctly. The fix is a capacity-map addition (mistral window + alias in core/pkg/capacity), not an adapter change.

The driver gap is the operational blocker that must close first: wait_turn (SEAM 2) and transcript-path resolution (SEAM 3) are stubbed in the scaffolded driver — record ports them from the claudecode reference driver before recording.

The daemon=full path for model display is confirmed by reading the parser + tailer, but is PROVISIONAL on emission until a live recording (the sidecar is rewritten every turn; the tailer must pick up the post-turn context_tokens). record promotes it from provisional to settled.
model_name is the agent's own normalized label; mistral-medium-3.5 is a concrete id (not a routing alias), so the 'auto counts' acceptance allowance is not exercised here.

sources.note: applySidecar sets ev.ModelName = NormalizeModelName(st.model) on every event and ev.Tokens = TokenSnapshot{Total: st.contextTokens} on turn_done, but does NOT set ContextWindow — the window is resolved downstream by the capacity map.
readSidecar decodes config.active_model -> model and stats.context_tokens -> contextTokens (and environment.working_directory -> cwd). No cost field is decoded — the adapter never emits cost.

The ONLY mistral entry in core/pkg/capacity is the CO2 tier ("mistral": {gCO2PerToken: 0.00285}). No context-window entry and no alias for mistral/medium/devstral/magistral — so GetModelCapacity("mistral-medium-3.5").ContextWindow == 0.

ComputeContextUtilization: with no override and GetModelCapacity(...).ContextWindow == 0, effectiveContextWindow <= 0 -> returns (0, 0, "unknown", true). context_window and context_utilization both 0 for mistral models — the context-bar half of the acceptance fails.
NormalizeModelName("mistral-medium-3.5") returns "mistral-medium-3.5" verbatim (verified by running it) — model_name is non-empty and != unknown, so the model-display half of the acceptance passes.

DRIVE_ELICITS="send slash sleep"; SEAM 2 wait_turn is a not_implemented stub; SEAM 3 (session_id+transcript path) is a TODO. recipe-lint's driver_elicits_from_file excludes wait_turn -> driver=gap:wait_turn.

Live surface: every sidecar carries config.active_model="mistral-medium-3.5" with stats.context_tokens populated (136662, 146631, 31309, ...) — confirms the agent exposes model + context from first contact and pins the model id from the live surface, not docs.
Acceptance requires model_name non-empty/!=unknown AND context_window > 0 AND context_utilization_percentage > 0 after the first turn — the context-bar bullets are what mistral-vibe fails today.

preconditions: vibe CLI (v2.19.0) on PATH and authenticated (MISTRAL_API_KEY or ~/.vibe/.env)
irrlichd is recording with the transcripts permission granted
driver SEAM 2 (wait_turn) and SEAM 3 (transcript-path resolution) ported from the reference driver before recording (driver-gap route)
setup: Launch vibe in a fresh empty cwd so a new ~/.vibe/logs/session/<id>/ is created
Assertions on model_name / context_window / context_utilization are checked AFTER the first turn_done; context_window/utilization are known_failing today (capacity-map gap)
verify: events.jsonl shows one ready->working->ready cycle on the session
SessionMetrics.ModelName after turn_done is 'mistral-medium-3.5' (non-empty, != unknown) — PASSES
SessionMetrics token count after turn_done is > 0 (context_tokens from the sidecar) — PASSES
SessionMetrics.ContextWindow after turn_done is > 0 — KNOWN_FAILING today (mistral absent from capacity map; ContextWindowUnknown=true)
SessionMetrics.ContextUtilization after turn_done is > 0 — KNOWN_FAILING today (window unresolved)
